items.scenario: You are a second-year student planning to enroll in an advanced statistics course next term. You noticed the catalog lists a prerequisite you have not taken, but you completed a similar course over the summer at another school.
items.direction: Write an email to Dr. Ferraro. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your background and the summer course you completed
Ask whether it satisfies the prerequisite
Explain why you want to take the advanced course now
items.subject: Prerequisite question for the advanced statistics course

items.scenario: You recently signed up for a weekend photography workshop run by the campus arts center. The confirmation email listed a different location than the one shown on the registration page, and you want to be sure where to go.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Sorensen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the conflicting location information you received
Describe which session you registered for
Suggest a way to confirm the correct details before Saturday
items.subject: Confirming the location for the weekend photography workshop

items.scenario: Your summer marketing internship ended two weeks ago, and your supervisor mentioned there might be part-time work during the school year. You have not heard anything since and would like to follow up while staying available.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Eklund. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank him for the experience during your internship
Ask whether any part-time positions are still open
Explain your availability for the coming semester
items.subject: Following up on possible part-time work this fall

items.scenario: You decided to start a small board-game night with friends every other week. One friend offered to host, but her apartment is far from campus, and a few people have said the trip is difficult without a car.
items.direction: Write an email to Mara. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the transportation problem some friends are facing
Suggest an alternative place or arrangement for hosting
Ask whether she still wants to host the first session
items.subject: Sorting out where to hold our board-game nights
